steps.title: Use the likely pattern
steps.content: The phrase "more likely" needs an infinitive complement: more likely to do something. The sentence is about the days when respondents were more likely to consume certain Brazilian foods.

steps.title: Choose the infinitive form
steps.content: Choice A is a past participle, which would make it sound as if respondents were consumed. Choice B is a finite past-tense verb, and choice D is a finite future-progressive phrase. The remaining choice supplies the correct infinitive: "more likely to eat." The answer is C.
